observation_summary: The CollectionUtils.removeAll method fails to remove the correct elements from the collection, resulting in a size of 2 instead of the expected 1.
hypothesis: The implementation of CollectionUtils.removeAll incorrectly invokes ListUtils.retainAll instead of ListUtils.removeAll, leading to an incorrect set of elements remaining in the collection.
prediction: The code for CollectionUtils.removeAll will contain a method call to ListUtils.retainAll(collection, remove) instead of ListUtils.removeAll(collection, remove).
experiment_rationale: The bug report explicitly states that the method calls the wrong utility function. This is a procedural error in the implementation of the method's logic.
reasoning_summary: The defect is a clear case of an incorrect method call within the implementation of CollectionUtils.removeAll. This is a procedural/algorithmic error where the wrong operation is performed to achieve the desired result. It is not a missing guard (Checking), a wrong value (Assignment), or a design-level capability omission (Function/Class/Object), but rather an incorrect implementation of the intended algorithm.

alternative_types.why_not_primary: While it involves calling a different method, the root cause is the internal implementation logic of CollectionUtils.removeAll, not a mismatch in the interface contract between two components.
